articles.headline: AI Leaders Weekly Briefing – Mistral AI and NVIDIA Form Nemotron Coalition for Open-Source Models
articles.summary: Mistral AI and NVIDIA have partnered with Thinking Machines Lab and Black Forest Labs to co-develop frontier-level open-source AI models through the Nemotron Coalition. Mistral also launched its Forge enterprise training platform to help companies build proprietary AI models. This initiative aims to challenge leading closed-source models like ChatGPT and Claude.

articles.headline: Mistral Releases Small 4: Open-Source Mixture-of-Experts Model with Multimodal Capabilities
articles.summary: Mistral unveiled Small 4, an open-source Apache-licensed model with 120 billion parameters, 256 token context, combining reasoning, vision, and agentic coding abilities. It features Magestral vision and pixel capabilities for multimodal tasks. This release bolsters Mistral's position in accessible AI development.

articles.headline: Elon Musk says xAI will match OpenAI, Google and Anthropic by 2026
articles.summary: Elon Musk has outlined an ambitious roadmap for xAI, stating it aims to reach the capabilities of leading AI labs like OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic by the end of 2026. He predicts xAI will surge ahead within three years, likening the gap to needing the James Webb Space Telescope to see competitors. The company is focusing on hiring, infrastructure improvements, and collaboration with SpaceX to meet computing demands.
